steps.title: Distribute the negative sign and group by degree
steps.content:
Distribute the negative sign across the second polynomial: $(6t^4 - 3t^2 + 3t - 6) - (2t^4 - 4t^3 + 6t^2 - 6)$ becomes $$6t^4 - 3t^2 + 3t - 6 - 2t^4 + 4t^3 - 6t^2 + 6$$ Group terms by degree:
  • $t^4$ terms: $6t^4 - 2t^4$
  • $t^3$ terms: $4t^3$
  • $t^2$ terms: $-3t^2 - 6t^2$
  • $t$ terms: $3t$
  • Constants: $-6 + 6$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Add each group:
  • $t^4$: $6t^4 - 2t^4 = 4t^4$
  • $t^3$: $4t^3$
  • $t^2$: $-3t^2 - 6t^2 = -9t^2$
  • $t$: $3t$
  • Constants: $-6 + 6 = 0$
The simplified expression is $4t^4 + 4t^3 - 9t^2 + 3t$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
